new_classes.definition_en: Concentrated grape must of heading 2204 30, as defined by Additional Note 7 to Chapter 22 of the Combined Nomenclature. This subheading covers grape must that has been concentrated (e.g. by evaporation), distinguishing it from grape must in fermentation or grape must with fermentation arrested otherwise than by alcohol (CN 2204 30 10).
A quality borne by a grape must indicating that it has undergone concentration (e.g. by partial evaporation of water), as specified in Additional Note 7 to Chapter 22 of the Combined Nomenclature.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for CN 2204 30 92 is very brief: it designates the subheading as "Concentrated" grape must and refers entirely to Additional Note 7 to Chapter 22 for the substantive definition. The distinguishing criterion captured here is: (1) the product is a grape must of heading 2204 30, (2) it is concentrated (modelled via ConcentrationQuality), and (3) it is distinguished from CN 2204 30 10 (grape must in fermentation or with fermentation arrested non-alcoholically). Coverage is partial (0.7) because the precise quantitative or qualitative thresholds defined in Additional Note 7 (e.g. specific Brix, density or dry extract values that define "concentrated") are not reproduced in the legal notes provided to this session, so those criteria cannot be fully axiomatised without the text of Additional Note 7.
